content.problemStatement:
In order to beat AlphaZero, Chef Ada is improving her chess calculation skills.

Today, Ada took a big chessboard with NNN rows (numbered 111 through NNN) and NNN columns (numbered 111 through NNN) and placed some pawns on this chessboard. Let's denote a cell in row rrr and column ccc of the chessboard by (r,c)(r, c)(r,c).

Ada may perform any number of moves in any order. In one move, Ada must take one pawn and capture another pawn with it; just like in standard chess, a pawn in a cell (r,c)(r, c)(r,c) may capture only pawns in cells (r−1,c−1)(r-1, c-1)(r−1,c−1) or (r−1,c+1)(r-1, c+1)(r−1,c+1). The captured pawn is removed and the pawn that captured it takes its place. It is not allowed to move pawns without capturing. Unlike in standard chess, the pawns do not have colours, so it is possible to use all pawns to capture and all pawns can be captured.

The number of moves Ada can make is finite, so the game can always reach a state where Ada has no valid moves. Ada is interested in the shortest possible sequence of moves such that there are no more valid moves afterwards. Help her find one.
constraints: the sum of N2N^2N2 over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
NNN lines follow. For each iii (1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N), the iii-th of these lines contains a single string with length NNN describing the iii-th row of the chessboard, where '.' denotes an empty cell and 'O' denotes a pawn.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case:


First, print a line containing a single integer MMM — the minimum number of moves.
Then, print MMM lines describing the moves in some shortest game. Each of these lines should contain two space-separated integers rrr and ccc followed by a space and a character ddd, denoting that the pawn in cell (r,c)(r, c)(r,c) captures the pawn in cell (r−1,c−1)(r-1, c-1)(r−1,c−1) if ddd is 'L' or that it captures the pawn in cell (r−1,c+1)(r-1, c+1)(r−1,c+1) if ddd is 'R'.
If there are multiple solutions, you may print any one.
